commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a non-interactive legacy cron-store migration during doctor config preflight, startup detection for default/custom legacy cron stores, and focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +224, Tests +170. Total +394 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and user reports, though this read-only review did not execute a live upgrade. Current main reads cron jobs from SQLite rows only while the linked issue reports intact legacy JSON stores after upgrade, matching the missing preflight migration path.

target.title: [codex] fix cron legacy migration
target.body:
Fixes #90072

## Summary

- Add a non-interactive cron auto-migration path that runs during doctor config preflight.
- Merge legacy JSON-only cron jobs into the SQLite cron store before runtime reads cron rows, while keeping existing SQLite rows as the winner for duplicate job ids.
- Archive legacy cron store files only after the SQLite write succeeds, and include default/custom legacy cron stores in lightweight startup detection for read-only preflight paths.

## Root Cause

Cron runtime now reads and writes the canonical SQLite-backed store only. The existing doctor cron repair flow could import legacy `jobs.json` / `jobs-state.json`, but ordinary startup/preflight paths did not call that cron migration before `gateway` or `cron list` touched runtime state. That made an upgrade appear to drop legacy jobs when SQLite already had only newly-created managed rows.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/cron/auto-migration.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.state-migration.test.ts src/cli/program/config-guard.test.ts src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --codex-bin /private/tmp/codex-autoreview-flex` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: legacy cron jobs are migrated into SQLite during config preflight before cron runtime/list/start paths rely on the SQLite-only store.

Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout with the repo Vitest harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/cron/auto-migration.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.state-migration.test.ts src/cli/program/config-guard.test.ts src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: the focused cron, CLI, and commands Vitest shards passed; `git diff --check` passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: the regression test imports a legacy-only cron job into SQLite, preserves the existing SQLite duplicate, removes legacy `jobId`/`notify`, migrates webhook delivery, and archives the legacy JSON file.

What was not tested: a live published-version upgrade from 2026.5.28 to 2026.6.1 with a real user cron store; GitHub CI is pending on this draft PR.
